title: <Short, action-oriented title>
notes:
DESIGN SPECIFICATION:
<Implementation details: what to change, how to change it, which patterns to follow>
Files to modify: <list of files>
Files to create: <list of files, if any>

_field_reference.id: Unique ticket identifier (e.g. JMAIL-009). Primary key.
_field_reference.title: Short action-oriented title describing the work.
_field_reference.feature_ref: Relative path to the feature specification markdown file.
_field_reference.status: One of: backlog, todo, building, in_progress, test, eval, merging, review, done.
_field_reference.last_updated: ISO 8601 timestamp, auto-set on every mutation.
_field_reference.dependencies: Array of ticket IDs that must reach review/done before this ticket can start.
_field_reference.acceptance_criteria: Array of objectively testable criteria. Agents are evaluated against these.
_field_reference.files_touched: Array of file paths created or modified by the implementation.
_field_reference.notes: Free-text design specification, implementation hints, and context for agents.
_field_reference.assignee: Agent assignment. String (tool name) or object { tool, model }. Null = unassigned.
_field_reference.agent: Runtime agent state. Set by scheduler when agent is running. Contains run_id, pid, state, stdout/stderr log paths.
_field_reference.eval_summary: Evaluation result object with verdict and per-criterion checks. Set by eval phase.
_field_reference.fail_count: Number of implementation failures (test phase failures). Integer.
_field_reference.eval_fail_count: Number of evaluation failures (eval phase failures). Integer.

_lifecycle.description: Active ticket file lifecycle — this JSON file exists on disk only during active execution phases.
_lifecycle.created: When updateTicket() transitions status to in_progress, test, eval, or merging.
_lifecycle.updated: All mutations during active phases write to this file (not DB).
_lifecycle.flushed: When status transitions to review — file data is upserted to DB and file is deleted.
_lifecycle.recovery: On startup, any orphaned ticket files are synced back to DB and deleted.
